fix: postbuild copies .next to repo root on Vercel for routes-manifest validator

The Vercel @vercel/next post-build step hardcodes /vercel/path0/.next/ when
checking routes-manifest-deterministic.json, even when rootDirectory=frontend.
With rootDirectory=frontend, Next.js builds to /vercel/path0/frontend/.next/.

Workaround: postbuild script copies .next/ to ../.next/ (repo root) after
build completes, only when VERCEL=1. The validator then finds the file.
No-op locally (VERCEL is unset) and on Railway (no VERCEL env var).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat(atlas): multi-asset carousel + context compression + system prompt guards

MULTI-ASSET CAROUSEL:
- CarouselPayload: backgrounds[] array (one per slide) instead of single backgroundBase64
- runAtlasCarousel: requests N backgroundPrompts with distinct compositions from LLM
- Generates N images in parallel via Promise.all (same ~20s as 1 image but N unique visuals)
- Auto-pads to N prompts if LLM returns fewer; fallback uses CONCEPTS[] rotation
- CreativeRenderer: bg = backgrounds[current] — each slide shows its own unique background

SYSTEM PROMPT GUARDS:
- TOKEN GUARD: focus on MASTER.md + client styleguide, ignore verbose docs
- IDENTITY LOCK: only #0C0C0E/#131317/#26262F/#F59E0B palette — reject blue/white/generic
- CARROSSEL DINAMICO: each slide MUST have unique visual concept (brain, interface, network, particles...)

CONTEXT COMPRESSION (dna-context.ts):
- MAX_DNA_CHARS=3000, MAX_STYLEGUIDE_CHARS=1800, MAX_PRODUCTS_CHARS=1200
- truncate() helper applied to every section before push
- Styleguide is highest priority (kept near full) — DNA and products compressed first
- Prevents 200k token overflow on clients with large knowledge files

commit.message: Inspector CLI (#18229) This change introduces the Inspector CLI. High level, the CLI includes a long lived node process for the "bridge", a short lived node process for the CLI (which communicates with the bridge), and a new `StartInspectable` API that runs in the browser/app side and connects to the bridge. `StartInspectable` uses parts of the Inspector framework, like the ServiceContainer for modularity/extensibility. Services can introduce additional commands, which are dynamically fetched from the CLI. The "inspectable" ServiceContainer is longer lived than the one in Inspector, and the one in Inspector is aware of the "inspectable" one, which means Inspector can launch the "inspectable" if needed, can expose bridge connection status, and even contribute more CLI commands. - Add the ability for `ServiceContainer` to have a "parent" `ServiceContainer` (longer lived). - Add `StartInspectable` that sets up a `ServiceContainer`with a set of services that are browser independent and provide a set of CLI commands (query scene entities, query entity details, take a screenshot, get stats, get shader code, etc.). - `ShowInspector` now calls `StartInspectable`, which is ref counted so if it has already been called, it just returns the existing instance. The inspectable `ServiceContainer` is used as a "parent" container for the Inspector `ServiceContainer`. - There is a new Inspector service that monitors the CLI "bridge" connection status and shows an icon in the bottom right toolbar, plus shows connect/disconnect toasts. - Added a CLI and CLI bridge (using WebSockets). There are a few fixed commands, like --help and --sessions, but everything else is dynamic and the commands are determined by the browser/app side. - Updated Inspector readme.md files to call out the CLI (largely for AI agent discoverability). - Updated the core readme.md (@babylonjs/core + babylonjs umd) with a tiny note about the Inspector (another breadcrumb for AI agents). - Updated the @babylonjs/inspector to fully bundle the CLI and the bridge, including all the node WebSocket code. This way it is very easy to run the CLI without having any external dependencies. - Marked new Inspectable related APIs as @experimental for now. When developing the Inspector CLI, you can run `npm run babylon-inspector -- --help` (or other commands) from the `packages/dev/inspector-v2` directory. If you've built the full monorepo, you can also run `npx babylon-inspector --help` (or other commands) from anywhere, which will pick up the final built CLI from the public package. This is the same way someone consuming the @babylonjs/inspector package would run the CLI. There is still an open question about whether we should have an Inspector MCP instead of or in addition to the CLI itself (most of the code would still stay the same though). Need to follow up with @RaananW on this. After this PR, I will also explore more the idea of calling `StartInspectable` by default in Sandbox and Playground, and also the idea of exposing additional tool specific CLI commands, like getting/setting Playground code for example. We need some more discussion around this to see how it relates to other tool MCP efforts, so this will be a possible future change. ``` [04/02 11:12:27] /Users/ryantremblay/Repos/Babylon.js/packages/dev/inspector-v2> npm run babylon-inspector -- --session 2 --command get-count-stats > @dev/inspector@1.0.0 babylon-inspector > node --no-warnings dist/cli/cli.js --bridge-script dist/cli/bridge.js --session 2 --command get-count-stats { "totalMeshes": 13, "activeMeshes": 13, "activeIndices": 1631379, "activeFaces": 543793, "activeBones": 6, "activeParticles": 30, "drawCalls": 2212014, "totalLights": 2, "totalVertices": 274644, "totalMaterials": 10, "totalTextures": 20 } ``` --------- Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

security: implement flood defenses — per-conn ratelimiter, ping ban, message gate

Four network-layer flood defenses against the tokio starvation attack (AV-14.11):

1. Per-connection rate limiter (server.rs)
   - handle_peer() now creates a local RateLimiter::new() that shadows the
     shared Arc<RwLock<RateLimiter>> parameter.
   - Eliminates the cross-peer write-lock contention that previously caused
     tokio thread starvation at 50+ simultaneous peers; each connection's
     rate checks are now fully independent.

2. Pre-channel message gate (server.rs, TLS + plaintext bridge tasks)
   - Both I/O bridge tasks now count raw messages per second before
     forwarding to the msg_read_tx channel (before deserialization).
   - Soft limit 200 msg/s: silently dropped (no channel send).
   - Hard limit 500 msg/s: sends Err('Message flood detected') to trigger
     record_violation() + record_message_flood() in the main loop, then
     closes the bridge task (disconnects the peer).

3. Ping flood escalation (server.rs)
   - ping_excess_streak counter: 3 consecutive pings that exceed the soft
     rate limit now call blacklist.record_violation() and
     ai.attack_detector.record_ping_flood().
   - If the violation count crosses the ban threshold, the peer is
     disconnected immediately.
   - Previously: excess pings silently dropped, peer never banned.

4. PingFlood + MessageFlood in AttackDetector (attack_detector.rs)
   - New AttackType variants: PingFlood, MessageFlood.
   - New fields: ping_flood_times, message_flood_times (sliding-window maps).
   - record_ping_flood(): ≥3 events in 10s → BlockPeer.
   - record_message_flood(): ≥2 events in 60s → BlockPeer (Critical severity).
   - Both feed the 30s enforcement loop → IPBlacklist auto-ban.

Fixes AV-14.11 (reconnection-storm tokio starvation) attack surface.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

- Make version dynamic in workspace members (remove hardcoded 0.1.0)
- Update Dockerfile to use uv build --wheel --all-packages for
  consistent VCS-derived versions across all workspace packages
- Update uv.lock to reflect dynamic workspace member versions

commit.message:
EXP-1301-1310: Response-curve ISF, UAM augmentation breakthrough, patient archetypes

Three breakthroughs in 10 experiments:

1. Response-curve ISF (EXP-1301): Exponential decay fitting yields R²=0.805
   fit quality with τ=2.0h. Avoids deconfounding degeneracy entirely.

2. Dawn detection (EXP-1302): First successful detection (2/4 patients)
   by comparing pre-dawn vs dawn glucose slopes directly.

3. UAM-augmented conservation (EXP-1309): Adding implicit UAM detection
   to physics model improves R² from -0.508 to +0.351 (Δ=+0.859).
   Physics model now explains more variance than constant prediction.

Also confirmed:
- Zero calm windows exist (EXP-1306): AID loop ALWAYS active
- 47% of timesteps violate conservation, 70% UAM-type (EXP-1305)
- 3 patient archetypes: well-calibrated(4), needs-tuning(6), miscalibrated(1)
- Only 1/9 patients has stable recommendations over 2-week windows

commit.message:
Fix corrupt GLB output: strip Draco metadata and consolidate data URIs after decompression

decompress_gltf() from gltf-draco-transcoder decompresses mesh binary data
but leaves KHR_draco_mesh_compression in the JSON metadata and stores
decompressed data as base64 data URIs in separate buffers. This produces
internally inconsistent GLBs that Godot cannot import.

Added _strip_draco_extension_from_glb() which:
- Removes KHR_draco_mesh_compression from extensionsUsed/Required
- Removes the extension from mesh primitive definitions
- Consolidates data-URI buffers back into the binary chunk
- Produces a spec-compliant single-buffer GLB

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix PAM service fallback: login -> sshd -> su

The 'login' PAM service includes pam_lastlog.so (missing on this system)
and has TTY requirements. 'sshd' uses @include common-auth which works
correctly for non-root programmatic PAM auth via unix_chkpwd (setgid shadow).

Service priority: /etc/pam.d/patchbox > sshd > su

Complete rewrite integrating 6 web engines under unified MCP interface:

Architecture:
- Engine Protocol with Capability enum (FETCH/SEARCH/INTERACT/STRUCTURED)
- EngineManager with SmartRouter + 5-layer fallback chain
- SiteRegistry: 67+ built-in sites with auto engine matching
- HealthMonitor: per-engine circuit breaker (closed→open→half-open)
- IntentClassifier: 9 query intent types, 130+ keyword patterns
- ResearchPipeline v3: intent→expand→discover→fetch→extract→quality→dedup→save

6 Engines:
- OpenCLI (100+ site adapters, structured data)
- Scrapling (3-tier HTTP: Fetcher→AsyncFetcher→StealthyFetcher)
- Lightpanda (Zig CDP browser, 9x speed, 16x less memory)
- PinchTab (remote browser MCP, session reuse)
- bb-browser (126+ adapters, most capable: FETCH+SEARCH+INTERACT+STRUCTURED)
- CLIBrowser (Rust stealth binary, ultimate fallback)

7 MCP Tools:
- research_and_collect (full pipeline, backward compatible)
- web_fetch (single URL, auto routing)
- web_cli (direct site commands)
- web_interact (browser automation)
- web_search (NEW: multi-engine search)
- web_crawl (NEW: BFS link-following crawl)
- engine_status (NEW: health check)

commit.message:
fix: set distDir='../.next' on Vercel to match post-build validator path

Vercel's post-build validator (introduced in recent @vercel/next update)
looks for routes-manifest-deterministic.json at /vercel/path0/.next/.
With rootDirectory:frontend, the build runs from /vercel/path0/frontend/
and outputs to frontend/.next — one directory below where the validator
looks. Setting distDir='../.next' when VERCEL=1 redirects the build output
to /vercel/path0/.next/ so the validator finds the file.

Locally VERCEL env var is not set so distDir defaults to .next (normal).

fix: word-wrap LLM prose at 80 chars, preserve tool output newlines

Two streaming modes:
- WordWrapStreamer: buffers LLM tokens, strips newlines, flushes at
  last word boundary before 80 chars. Each line gets prefix once.
- LineStreamer: flushes on \n for deterministic tool output (unused
  in current event handlers but available for future use).

Tool results still use truncate() for compact single-line display.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(stt): startup hardware validation and binary metadata tracking

- Add .server-info.json metadata written after each server download
  (stores backend, GPU vendor, source repo, asset name, timestamp)
- Validate server binary compatibility at app startup via
  validateAndCleanIncompatibleBinary() — auto-deletes mismatched binaries
- Add proactive check in SttService._start() before launching subprocess
- Enhanced isServerBinaryCompatible() with two-layer validation:
  1. Metadata-based backend comparison (e.g., cpu vs vulkan)
  2. DLL heuristic for CUDA/non-NVIDIA mismatch (Windows)
- Detects both directions: GPU binary on CPU system AND CPU binary on
  GPU-capable system (sub-optimal performance)
- deleteServerBinary() now also cleans up the .server-info.json file
